subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Mushroom Poisoning
description: Learn Mushroom Poisoning in one minute.

hook.text: The death cap mushroom looks harmless, tastes pleasant, and kills in days. It has no antidote.

buildup.text: Amanita phalloides causes 90% of mushroom poisoning deaths worldwide.

discovery.text: Its toxin—amatoxin—destroys liver cells by blocking RNA production inside them.

twist.text: Symptoms don't appear for 6-12 hours, by which time liver damage is already severe.

climax.text: The only reliable rule: never eat a wild mushroom you haven't identified with 100% certainty.

punchline.text: It looks like dinner. It tastes like dinner. It's the last dinner.

quiz.question: Why is the death cap mushroom so deadly?
options: Symptoms are delayed and liver damage occurs before you feel sick
